Introduction to HTTP

HTTP is the foundation of communication on the web.

“HTTP is the protocol that powers data communication across the World Wide Web.”
World Wide Web Consortium

Published on August 4, 2025

Modern applications depend on APIs, HTTP requests, and secure communication to exchange information.

HTTP Methods

Common HTTP Request Methods
Method Purpose Description
GET Retrieve Fetch resources from a server
POST Create Send new data to the server
PUT Update Replace an existing resource
DELETE Remove Delete a resource from the server
HTTP methods define actions between clients and servers.

HTTP Status Codes

Category Code Meaning
Success 200 OK
201 Created
Client Error 400 Bad Request
404 Not Found
Server Error 500 Internal Server Error
503 Service Unavailable

HTTP Request Simulator

API Request Form







Request ready to be submitted.
Error: Invalid endpoint URL.

HTTP Multimedia Resources





Frequently Asked Questions

What is HTTP?

HTTP is the protocol used for communication between web clients and servers.

What does status code 404 mean?

It means the requested resource could not be found.

What is an API?

APIs allow software systems to communicate with each other.
